metaBuilds.title: Magical Bruiser (Physical-Def)
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 91 auto-attack DPS in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Enchanter
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for heal/shield output, online around minute 27 — about 365 HP healed or shielded over 10s in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +36% more heal/shield output: try Oathkeeper over Megacosm (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Mixed-Def Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 7,165 effective HP vs physical in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 28% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Adele deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite; you give up 22% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 26% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Riktor ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 23% of its bite; you give up 22% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 28% of its bite; you give up 22% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Narbash deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 28% of its bite; you give up 23% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Nightstalker” ⇒ poke; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field support 52.8% over 119,747 games, magnitude not simulated.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 28% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Bayle deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.
